preamble: Palatalization of the voiceless dental/alveolar stop /t/ to the voiceless postalveolar affricate [tʃ] is a common phonetic process. This typically occurs before high front vowels like /i/, where the tongue body raises towards the palate.
sources: Vance, T. J. (2008). The Sounds of Japanese.
Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
